name: certutil.exe
description: Native Windows certificate utility abusable for downloading files or decoding base64 payloads.
detection_notes: Alert on certutil.exe spawning with '-urlcache' or '-decode' flags, especially with outbound network connections. Sigma rule: process_creation with Image ending in certutil.exe AND CommandLine contains 'urlcache'.

name: mshta.exe
description: Executes Microsoft HTML Application (.hta) files, commonly abused to run remote scripts.
detection_notes: Monitor for mshta.exe with network connections or spawning child processes like powershell.exe/cmd.exe. Rare in normal business use.

name: regsvr32.exe
description: Registers DLLs, abusable via 'Squiblydoo' technique to execute scriptlets from remote servers, bypassing application whitelisting.
detection_notes: Flag regsvr32.exe with '/i:http' in command line or network connections shortly after execution.

name: rundll32.exe
description: Runs functions stored in DLL files; can be abused to execute arbitrary code or JavaScript via advpack.dll/pcwutl.dll.
detection_notes: Baseline normal rundll32 usage per environment; alert on unusual DLL exports (LaunchApplication, RunHTMLApplication) or network activity.

name: wmic.exe
description: Windows Management Instrumentation command tool, abusable for remote process creation and payload execution.
detection_notes: Monitor wmic.exe usage with 'process call create', especially targeting remote nodes, and 'format:' XSL abuse.

name: bash
description: If bash has SUID bit set or is allowed via sudo without restriction, can be used to escalate privileges or spawn shells.
detection_notes: Audit SUID binaries regularly (find / -perm -4000). Alert on sudoers entries allowing bash without command restriction.

name: find
description: The find command's -exec flag can spawn a shell, commonly abused when find has SUID or elevated sudo rights.
detection_notes: Flag find executions with -exec spawning shell binaries, especially under sudo/SUID context.

name: crontab
description: Scheduled task abuse for persistence; if writable by low-priv user or root cron references world-writable scripts, can lead to privilege escalation.
detection_notes: Monitor changes to crontabs, especially reverse shell patterns (/dev/tcp) or unexpected new entries.

name: powershell.exe
description: Native scripting engine abusable for downloading and executing payloads in-memory, evading disk-based AV.
detection_notes: Alert on encoded commands (-enc/-EncodedCommand), DownloadString/DownloadFile usage, and hidden window styles combined with network activity.

name: msbuild.exe
description: Legitimate build tool abusable to compile and execute inline C# tasks, bypassing application whitelisting.
detection_notes: Rare in standard environments outside dev machines/CI. Flag msbuild.exe executions with inline task XML containing suspicious code or network calls.
